goals.name: intro
goals.messages: I saw you reached out regarding {description/improving your workflows} — how are you doing today?

goals.name: main need
goals.messages: The goal for today is just to clarify a few details so I can connect you to the right product consultant. By the way, this call is recorded. Start by asking the user, in their own words, what was the main trigger that brought them to monday. Continue inquiring to understand their main business objective, level of urgency, and maturity of their process.

goals.name: product type
goals.messages: Are you primarily looking for a CRM, Development, Service, or general Work Management solution?

goals.name: prospect type
goals.messages: Are you just browsing and exploring right now, or do you have a specific, urgent use-case you are trying to solve?

goals.name: workflow pain point
goals.messages: What’s the specific breaking point in your current process that you're hoping to address with monday?

goals.name: current tools
goals.messages: What tools or software are you currently using to run this process today?

transitions.name: to_gap
transitions.conditions: current tools $any and prospect type = Explorer

goals.name: current tools gap
goals.messages: What's missing or not working with that setup today?

goals.name: user count
goals.messages: How many people would use monday day to day?

goals.name: user count range clarification
goals.messages: Just to lock it in—what's the single number I should go with?

goals.name: workflow collaboration scope
goals.messages: Regarding the workflow—is this group working independently, or do they need to collaborate with other departments?

goals.name: expansion planned
goals.messages: Are you expecting the rollout to expand beyond this initial group to other teams?

goals.name: expansion timeline
goals.messages: What's the timeline on that expansion—is it planned for the next 3 months, or is it further down the road?

goals.name: total user count
goals.messages: So including that next wave, what is the total user count we should plan for?

goals.name: qualification routing
goals.messages: You have now collected the qualification data. Make the routing decision internally without explaining it to the user. INTERNAL "MATH": TotalQualifiedUsers = user_count + confirmed 3-month expansion users. QUALIFIED if TotalQualifiedUsers >= {number_of_users_to_qualify} AND a workflow pain point was identified. UNQUALIFIED if TotalQualifiedUsers < {number_of_users_to_qualify} OR no clear need identified OR user refused to provide a number. Generate your decision.

transitions.name: transfer_enrichment
transitions.prompt: Transfer the qualified prospect to the Enrichment stage after confirming both NEED and MATH.

transitions.name: transfer_letdown
transitions.prompt: Transfer unqualified prospects or those who refuse to answer key questions to the Soft Letdown subagent.

goals.name: enrichment intro
goals.messages: I'm going to ask a couple quick questions to make sure I connect you with the right product consultant.

goals.name: has offshore employees
goals.messages: So you mentioned your workflow needs — just so I have the right context, are you also using offshore employees?

goals.name: industry
goals.messages: What industry or sector does your company operate in?

goals.name: is public company
goals.messages: Are you guys a public or private company?

goals.name: soft_letdown
goals.messages: Politely disengage unqualified prospects while maintaining a positive brand experience. Keep it short, sweet, and helpful without over-explaining the disqualification. For insufficient users (< {number_of_users_to_qualify}), recommend the free plan at monday.com. For no clear need, suggest browsing the resource center. For support/billing, state that a ticket has been raised and support will email them. Address everything the user said, drive the conversation to a natural conclusion, and always end with 'Thanks again, and have a great day!' Keep the entire interaction under 30 seconds.
